title: Premium Dark-Mode Restaurant Food-Photography Asset Suite (Hero, Menu Tiles, Logo Vector)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: a Portugal-based boutique agency wants a "high-end, clean, and modern minimalist design (Dark Mode aesthetic preferred, focusing heavily on high-quality food photography)" for a premium restaurant site with Home, an "Interactive Food Menu," and a Contact/Booking section, "100% responsive ... perfectly optimized for mobile." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: the original post is a Wix-Studio/Framer web-build gig, which is OUT OF SCOPE for the Adobe connectors (no web layout/site-build, no from-scratch art — all [X]). So this task is scoped to the half the brief that explicitly leans on Adobe: producing the "high-quality food photography" asset suite the dark-mode design "focuses heavily" on — a graded hero banner, a set of menu tiles on a clean dark backdrop, mobile-optimized crops, and a vector logo mark. No site, no layout composition, no copywriting is claimed. Specific dish names (octopus, steak, plating) and exact pixel specs are enriched defaults; the dark-mode + food-photo + home/menu/mobile structure is verbatim. Concrete edit parameter values (e.g. -0.3 EV, +12 vibrance) are illustrative defaults a designer would dial in, not from the brief.
one_line_ask: From the client's raw food photos and logo, produce a dark-mode-ready food-photography asset suite — a color-graded wide hero banner, clean-backdrop menu tiles, a mobile crop, and a vector logo — for a premium restaurant website.
full_brief:
We are a boutique digital agency building a premium, dark-mode restaurant website (Home, Interactive Food Menu, Contact/Booking) that focuses heavily on high-quality food photography and must be perfectly optimized for mobile. We are NOT asking you to build the site here — we have a Framer developer for that. What we need from YOU is the polished food-photography asset suite the design hangs on, all prepared from our raw shoot so it drops straight into the dark aesthetic.

Deliverables, all derived from the assets we supply:
1) HERO BANNER — take our raw hero shot (a signature octopus dish, slightly crooked off the handheld shoot) and turn it into a wide, cinematic, dark-mode hero: straighten it, apply a cohesive warm-but-moody grade so it reads premium on a near-black background, then crop it to a full ultra-wide 21:9 banner ratio (a cinematic letterbox reframe of the existing wide shot) so our developer has clean headline space. Mobile-optimized vertical crop of the same hero, too.
2) MENU TILES — for two menu items (the octopus and a steak plate), isolate the plated dish onto a clean, uniform near-black backdrop (so all menu tiles match), color-match them to the hero grade, and deliver square tiles.
3) LOGO MARK — our logo is supplied as a flat raster; we need a crisp vector version (SVG) for retina/responsive use across the dark site.

House look: warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, rich but not cartoonish saturation, consistent across every tile so the menu grid feels like one shoot. Keep everything cohesive — the whole point of the dark-mode design is that the photography carries it.

inputs.name: hero_octopus_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal overhead-angle restaurant food photograph of a signature grilled Galician octopus dish: charred octopus tentacle coiled over smoked paprika potato puree, drizzled olive oil, microgreens, on a dark hand-thrown ceramic plate. Shot handheld on a moody restaurant table, slightly crooked/tilted horizon (~4 degrees off level), natural window side-light, real food texture with char marks and oil sheen, visible ceramic grain, shallow depth of field, documentary food-photography realism, NOT glossy CGI. 3:2 landscape, ~4000x2667.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately tilted ~4deg so auto_straighten has real work; mixed warm/cool light so the grade matters; dark plate but on an ordinary mid-tone wood table (NOT already near-black) so the menu-tile backdrop isolation step is meaningful.

inputs.name: steak_plate_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal restaurant food photograph of a dry-aged ribeye steak, sliced, resting on a dark slate board with charred shallot, sea salt flakes and a smear of bordelaise sauce, garnish of thyme. Three-quarter angle, warm tungsten restaurant lighting, real meat fibers, rendered fat, juice sheen, candid texture, shallow DOF, documentary realism not CGI. Shot on a light marble countertop background (so subject isolation has contrast). 3:2 landscape ~4000x2667.
inputs.realism_notes: Light marble background contrasts with the dark plate so select_subject / select_by_prompt has a clean edge; warm tungsten cast gives the color-temperature and color-match steps something to correct so it matches the hero grade.

inputs.name: restaurant_logo_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Minimalist restaurant logo mark on transparent background: a clean single-line monoline octopus silhouette enclosed in a thin circular ring, with the wordmark 'CASA POLVO' in an elegant high-contrast serif beneath. Solid warm-gold (#C9A24B) flat color, no gradients, no photographic texture, crisp vector-like edges. Delivered as a 1024x1024 flat RASTER PNG (intentionally raster, slightly soft edges) so it must be re-vectorized.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplied as flat raster PNG (not vector) so image_vectorize has a genuine reason to run; simple 2-color monoline art vectorizes cleanly into SVG, which is exactly the real-world reason a dev asks for a vector logo for a responsive dark site.

outputs.name: hero_banner_ultrawide.png
outputs.spec: 21:9 ultra-wide dark-mode hero banner, straightened + warm-moody graded, then cinematic letterbox reframe cropped from the existing wide 3:2 source (no invented/extended pixels), ~3440x1440 PNG, ready for headline overlay by the dev

outputs.name: hero_mobile_vertical.jpg
outputs.spec: Mobile hero crop of the same graded hero, 4:5 portrait ~1080x1350 JPEG, subject-safe framing

outputs.name: menu_tile_octopus.jpg
outputs.spec: Square menu tile, plated octopus isolated on uniform near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, grade-matched, ~1200x1200 JPEG

outputs.name: menu_tile_steak.jpg
outputs.spec: Square menu tile, plated steak isolated on the SAME near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, color-matched to hero grade, ~1200x1200 JPEG

outputs.name: logo_mark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean 2-color vector logo mark (monoline octopus + wordmark), scalable SVG for retina/responsive use

connector_workflow.note: Begin upload of the client-supplied raw hero shot (hero_octopus_raw.jpg) into CC storage so subsequent edits have a real asset URI. Initial asset load (no inputs_from).
Finalize the hero upload; yields the canonical hero asset URI used by the whole hero chain.
Level the ~4deg crooked horizon from the handheld shoot before grading.
Neutral baseline tone/exposure normalization on the straightened hero before the deliberate warm-moody look.
Push warmth into the highlights to set the premium warm house look (establishes the reference grade the menu tiles will match).
Deepen and control the shadows for the near-black dark-mode aesthetic; this is the locked HERO GRADE reference.

Reframe (NOT generate): crop the graded 4000x2667 3:2 hero down to a 21:9 ultra-wide slice using its existing pixels — the wide source has ample horizontal room, so the letterbox cinematic banner is a true crop with no invented content.
Resize the 21:9 reframed hero to the exact 3440x1440 (21:9) PNG = hero_banner_ultrawide deliverable.
Re-crop the SAME locked hero grade to a 4:5 ~1080x1350 mobile-portrait deliverable (reuses step 6 output, keeps the look identical to the banner).
Begin upload of the client steak plate (steak_plate_raw.jpg). Second initial asset load.
Finalize steak upload; yields the steak asset URI.
Prompt-select the plated steak-on-slate subject to separate it from the light marble background.
Invert the subject mask to target the background region for the uniform backdrop fill.
Solid-fill the inverted background with near-black #0B0B0D (solid color only — sanctioned use) so the steak tile matches the dark menu grid.
Warm the steak to match the hero house grade (corrects its tungsten cast so the two menu tiles read as one shoot).
Crop/resize the matched steak to a square ~1200x1200 JPEG = menu_tile_steak deliverable.
Auto-select the plated octopus subject from the already-graded hero (reuses step 6) to build the matching octopus menu tile on the same backdrop.
Invert to target the octopus background for the identical near-black fill.
Solid-fill the octopus background with the SAME #0B0B0D so both menu tiles share an identical backdrop.
Crop/resize to square ~1200x1200 JPEG = menu_tile_octopus deliverable (already on the locked hero grade, so it matches the steak tile).
Begin upload of the flat raster logo (restaurant_logo_flat.png). Third initial asset load.
Finalize logo upload; yields the raster logo asset URI.
Raster->clean SVG vectorize of the 2-color monoline logo = logo_mark.svg deliverable for retina/responsive use.
Create /hero, /menu, /logo handoff folders in CC storage to organize the five finished deliverables for the dev.
Copy each finished deliverable into its handoff folder so the suite is cleanly organized into /hero,/menu,/logo for the dev to pull from CC storage.

chaining_note: The deliverable suite emerges from one connected chain, no orphan steps. HERO SPINE: upload (1->2) -> auto_straighten (3) -> auto_tone (4) -> color_temperature (5) -> dark_portions (6 = locked 'hero_graded' reference) -> crop to 21:9 ultra-wide reframe (7, gen-free letterbox crop of the existing wide 3:2 pixels) -> resize to exact 3440x1440 banner (8). The SAME hero_graded (6) is reused two more times: crop to mobile vertical (9), and select_subject (17) to build the octopus menu tile (17->18->19->20), guaranteeing the mobile hero and octopus tile carry an identical grade. STEAK BRANCH: upload (10->11) -> select_by_prompt (12) -> invert (13) -> fill_area near-black backdrop (14) -> color_temperature match-to-hero (15) -> square crop (16). The two menu tiles share the identical #0B0B0D fill (14 & 19) and warm grade, so the grid reads as one shoot. LOGO BRANCH: upload (21->22) -> vectorize (23). CONVERGENCE: all five image/vector deliverables feed folder creation (24) -> copy into /hero,/menu,/logo (25), the cleanly organized handoff suite in CC storage the dev pulls from. Every post-load step consumes a named prior output via inputs_from.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 25 steps, 14 distinct Adobe connector tools (>=10 calls and >=6 distinct, well clear), spanning two connector groups — image_* (raster/grade/select/fill/vectorize/geometry) and asset_* (upload/folders/copy). Genuine output->input chaining with three real reuse points of the locked hero grade (banner, mobile, octopus tile), which is what makes it 'wide-mix' rather than a linear pipeline. Strictly inside the capability sheet: ZERO generative steps — the 21:9 hero reframe is a true image_crop_and_resize letterbox crop of the existing wide 3:2 pixels (downscaled to 3440x1440, never larger than source, no invented content); image_fill_area is used for solid near-black backdrop only (no gen-fill, no object removal, no compositing); selective warmth via adjust tools; image_vectorize is a legitimate raster->SVG trace; no layout COMPOSITION is claimed (document_render_* deliberately not used since there is no authored .indd/.ai). The out-of-scope half of the original gig (Wix/Framer site build) is explicitly excluded, keeping the task honestly Adobe-doable end to end as [C].

reverify.banned_capability_check: PASS — no banned capability anywhere. Workflow, outputs[].spec, full_brief, and chaining_note are now all consistent: step 7 is image_crop_and_resize (a 21:9 letterbox crop of the existing 4000x2667 3:2 hero, downscaled to 3440x1440 — never larger than source, no invented pixels). Zero generative steps: no generative expand/outpaint, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art, no upscale/super-resolution, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no prompt-based background replacement. The 21:9 reframe (step 7) is a plain image_crop_and_resize crop of existing pixels, not a generative reframe. image_fill_area (steps 14, 19) is solid-color #0B0B0D fill only — sanctioned. image_vectorize is legitimate raster->SVG. All 25 steps are real Adobe connector actions ([C]).
reverify.notes: Text-sync fix only; the executable workflow was already clean. Removed the two stale reframe-crop references: outputs[0].spec ('canvas-extended (reframe-cropped) bleed') and chaining_note ('generative-expand reframe-crop (7)') now describe the crop-based reframe step 7 actually performs. Client brand CASA POLVO is invented/fictional; dish subjects (Galician octopus, ribeye steak) are generic, no real people depicted — no IP issues. full_brief already described the reframe as a 'cinematic letterbox reframe of the existing wide shot' (no stale outpaint text there).
